title: Should we name the office robot?
summary: Two coworkers in an office argue about whether to give a name to the cleaning robot.

body.speaker: Anh
body.text: Look, the new robot is cleaning the floor again. It's so cute.

body.speaker: Anh
body.text: I know. But the team wants to give it a name. What do you think?

body.speaker: Binh
body.text: Why? It's a thing. We don't name our printer.

body.speaker: Anh
body.text: Well, our printer doesn't move. The robot comes to my desk every day. I almost said 'good morning' to it on Monday.

body.speaker: Binh
body.text: And that is the problem. If we name it, we will start to think it has feelings. It doesn't.

body.speaker: Anh
body.text: I know it doesn't. But a name is also for us, not only for it.

body.speaker: Binh
body.text: Hmm. What name did they choose?

body.speaker: Binh
body.text: And one day, when Bao breaks, you will feel sad. You will say 'poor Bao'. You will be slow to throw it away.

body.speaker: Anh
body.text: Maybe. But maybe that is good. Maybe we should be slow to throw things away.

body.speaker: Binh
body.text: …I did not think of it like that.

grammarNotes.title: Will for predictions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will' để dự đoán điều có thể xảy ra: 'You will feel sad', 'You will be slow to throw it away'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will' for predictions: 'You will feel sad', 'You will be slow to throw it away'.

cloze.template:
Anh: Look, the new robot is cleaning the floor again. It's so {{b1}}.
Binh: Cute? It's a {{b2}}, Anh.
Anh: I know. But the team wants to give it a {{b3}}. What do you think?
Binh: Why? It's a thing. We don't name our {{b4}}.
Anh: Well, our printer doesn't move. The robot comes to my desk {{b5}} day. I almost said 'good morning' to it on Monday.
Binh: And that is the problem. If we name it, we will {{b6}} to think it has feelings. It doesn't.
Anh: I know it doesn't. But a name is also for us, not only for {{b7}}.
Binh: Hmm. What name did they choose?
Anh: Someone said 'Bao'. It means 'safe' or '{{b8}}'.
Binh: And one day, when Bao breaks, you will feel sad. You will say 'poor Bao'. You will be slow to {{b9}} it away.
Anh: Maybe. But maybe that is good. Maybe we should be slow to throw things away.
Binh: …I did not think of it like {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: Anh thinks the robot is cute.
The team wants to give it a name.
'we will start to think it has feelings.'
'I did not think of it like that.'

translationVi:
Anh: Nhìn kìa, con robot mới đang lau sàn nữa kìa. Dễ thương quá.
Bình: Dễ thương á? Nó là cái máy mà, Anh.
Anh: Tôi biết. Nhưng cả đội muốn đặt tên cho nó. Cậu nghĩ sao?
Bình: Tại sao? Nó là đồ vật. Mình có đặt tên cho máy in đâu.
Anh: Ờ, máy in đâu có di chuyển. Con robot này đến bàn tôi mỗi ngày. Thứ Hai vừa rồi tôi suýt chào nó 'chào buổi sáng'.
Bình: Đấy chính là vấn đề. Đặt tên rồi mình sẽ bắt đầu nghĩ nó có cảm xúc. Nó đâu có.
Anh: Tôi biết nó không có. Nhưng cái tên cũng là cho mình, không chỉ cho nó.
Bình: Hừm. Người ta chọn tên gì?
Anh: Có người đề nghị 'Bao'. Nghĩa là 'an toàn' hay 'được bảo vệ'.
Bình: Rồi một ngày khi Bao hỏng, cậu sẽ thấy buồn. Cậu sẽ nói 'tội nghiệp Bao'. Cậu sẽ chậm chạp khi vứt nó đi.
Anh: Có thể. Nhưng có thể như vậy lại tốt. Có thể chúng ta nên chậm hơn khi vứt đồ đi.
Bình: …tôi không nghĩ theo hướng đó.

questions.prompt: What is Anh's first opinion about the robot?
Why does Binh not want to name it?
questions.explanation: 'It's a thing. We don't name our printer.'

questions.prompt: What did Anh almost do to the robot?
questions.explanation: 'I almost said good morning to it on Monday.'

questions.prompt: Binh thinks giving a name will make people…
questions.explanation: 'If we name it, we will start to think it has feelings.'

questions.hint: Look for a short name in quotes.

questions.prompt: What does Binh predict will happen when the robot breaks?
options: They will feel sad and be slow to throw it away
They will buy a better one
questions.explanation: 'When Bao breaks, you will feel sad…'

questions.prompt: How does Anh answer Binh's prediction?
options: He says maybe being slow to throw things away is good
He says he will buy a new one
questions.explanation: 'Maybe we should be slow to throw things away.'
questions.hint: Anh turns Binh's worry into a positive.

questions.prompt: How does Binh feel at the end?
options: Surprised — he did not think of it that way
questions.explanation: 'I did not think of it like that.'

questions.prompt: What is the main question of the dialogue?
options: Should we treat things with care, even if they have no feelings?
questions.explanation: Naming the robot is really about how we treat things.
questions.hint: Look past the robot — what is the deeper question?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Anh says naming the robot is for the humans, not for the robot. Do the small ways we treat objects say something about the kind of people we are?
